Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user wants a DuckDB SQL query to filter out cases grouped by case id that contain more than six events The table is event log with columns case id activity timestamp

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Steps 1 Group by case id and count the number of events per case 2 Keep only case ids where the count is less than or equal to 6 3 Select all original columns for these cases

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Query options Option 1 Using a subquery in WHERE clause

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Option 1 is generally more efficient and straightforward I will provide Option 1 as the primary solution and maybe mention Option 2 Lets provide a clean efficient query

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait the prompt asks to craft a DuckDB SQL query that filters out any cases containing more than six events and returns only the events from cases that have six or fewer events in total Using HAVING COUNT star less than or equal to 6 is the standard way
